drowned_channeler.name: Drowned Channeler
description: ## Drowned Channeler
‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/52›

When a drowned spirit doomed to a watery grave descends toward the evil of undeath and seeks refuge in a mortal shell, the result is an unusual spiritualist with powers that resonate with the drowned spirit's demise.
::cskill{gain=swim lose=fly}

::ab[Drowned Phantom]{icon=power-down ability="A drowned channeler has a *drowned phantom.* A *drowned phantom's* ectoplasmic body drips with water and seems to bleed off watery droplets even its incorporeal form. A *drowned phantom* gains a swim speed of 30 feet in both forms and gains the ‹subtype/water› subtype in addition to the phantom subtype, meaning that it can use its natural slam attacks underwater without taking the penalty for bludgeoning attacks underwater. The drowned channeler's *etheric tether* extends twice as far underwater, but only half as far above water. Additionally, the *drowned phantom* doesn't grant any of the usual benefits while confined in the drowned channeler's consciousness unless the drowned channeler is within 25 feet of an ocean, sea, lake, or other large body of water. Both forms of *bonded manifestation* grant the *drowned phantom's* swim speed of 30 feet to the drowned channeler, and attacks from the ectoplasmic *bonded manifestation's* tendrils don't take the typical penalties for bludgeoning attacks underwater, as if the phantom itself made them." alter="Phantom~shared consciousness~etheric tether~bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Drowned Spells]{l=2 icon=learn ability="A drowned channeler adds ‹spell/control water› (4th), ‹spell/fluid form› (6th), ‹spell/hydraulic push› (1st), ‹spell/hydraulic torrent› (3rd), ‹spell/life current› (3rd), ‹spell/pressure adaptation› (3rd), ‹spell/ride the waves› (4th), ‹spell/slipstream› (2nd), ‹spell/stabilize pressure› (2nd), ‹spell/unlife current› (3rd), ‹spell/water breathing› (3rd), and ‹spell/wave form› (4th) to the spiritualist spell list at the listed spell level. (This is the level they appear on the cleric list or sorcerer/wizard list, whichever is lower.)" special="The drowned channeler must still select them as spells known as normal." replace="Bonded senses"}

::ab[Drowned Powers (Sp)]{icon=magic l5="A drowned channeler can use *hydraulic push* as a spell-like ability once per day." incrementMulti="She can use *hydraulic push* ~ times a day.~5/4;2/1~~She can use *slipstream* ~ times a day.~7/4;2/1" imp7="She can use *slipstream* once per day as a spell-like ability." imp9="She can use *ride the waves* once per day as a spell-like ability." imp16="She can use *fluid form* once per day as a spell-like ability." replace="Detect undead~calm spirit~see invisibility~call spirit"}

ectoplasmatist.name: Ectoplasmatist
description: ## Ectoplasmatist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/108›
Instead of calling upon a phantom from the Ethereal Plane, an ectoplasmatist infuses herself with the mysterious substance called ectoplasm.

::ab[Ectoplasmic Lash (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee fullround="An ectoplasmatist can manifest one or two lashes of ectoplasm tethered to her by wispy, ectoplasmic tendrils. If she manifests two lashes, she can wield them both as light melee weapons, each dealing 1d6 points of slashing damage (1d4 if the ectoplasmatist is Small) with a critical threat range and multiplier of 19-20/&times&2. If the ectoplasmatist manifests only one *lash,* she can manifest it either as a single one-handed melee weapon that deals 1d8 points of slashing damage (1d6 if the ectoplasmatist is Small) with a critical threat range and multiplier of 19-20/&times&2, or as a two-handed melee weapon that deals 2d6 points of bludgeoning damage (1d10 if the ectoplasmatist is Small) with a critical multiplier of &times&2. Only the ectoplasmatist can wield or use these lashes. Dismissing any or all manifested lashes is a @HLfree_action." increment="The lashes' enhancement bonus~2~4" imp2="The ectoplasmatist's lashes gain a +1 enhancement bonus on attack and damage rolls, and are treated as magic weapons." imp4="The ectoplasmatist can use her lashes as ‹misc/reach› weapons or use them to manipulate objects from a distance. When used as weapons, the lashes gain an additional 5 feet of reach. Unlike most other weapons with reach, the lashes can be used to attack foes anywhere within the ectoplasmatist's reach (including adjacent foes). Attacks with the lashes' extended reach provoke attacks of opportunity just as if the ectoplasmatist were attacking with a ranged weapon. When the lashes are used to manipulate items, they have the same manual dexterity as the ectoplasmatist's hands. Using the lashes to manipulate items always provokes attacks of opportunity, even if the action the ectoplasmatist is taking normally wouldn't." imp6="The lashes are treated as having the ectoplasmatist's alignment for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp8="The ectoplasmatist's lashes become ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› weapons and their reach increases by another 5 feet (for a total increase of 10 feet). Additionally, attacks with the lashes against non-adjacent foes no longer provoke attacks of opportunity. Using the lashes to manipulate objects from a distance still provokes attacks of opportunity, but only if the action the ectoplasmatist is taking would normally provoke such attacks (instead of always provoking)." imp12="The ectoplasmatist chooses a single ‹ability/emotional focus› (‹emotionalfocus/anger›, ‹emotionalfocus/dedication›, ‹emotionalfocus/despair›, ‹emotionalfocus/fear›, ‹emotionalfocus/hatred›, ‹emotionalfocus/jealousy›, ‹emotionalfocus/kindness›, ‹emotionalfocus/remorse›, ‹emotionalfocus/whimsey›, or ‹emotionalfocus/zeal›). The ectoplasmatist's lashes gain a special weapon ability as if affected by a ‹spell/spirit-bound blade› spell matching the emotional energy of the focus the ectoplasmatist has chosen." imp16="The ectoplasmatist chooses a second *emotional focus,* and her lashes gain the special weapon abilities as if affected by a *spirit-bound blade* spell with the special weapon ability of both of the ectoplasmatist's chosen *emotional foci.*" replace="Etheric tether~phantom~shared consciousness~fused consciousness~spiritual bond~empowered consciousness"}

::ab[Spiritual Combat (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic order=ra fullround="An ectoplasmatist can make all of her attacks with a single light or one-handed *ectoplasmic lash* wielded in one hand, and also cast a single spiritualist spell with a casting time of one standard action as a @HLfree_action either before or after making the *ectoplasmic lash* attacks. The ectoplasmatist takes a -2 penalty on her attack rolls when using *spiritual combat.* If she decides to ‹rule/cast «the spell »defensively›, she can choose to take an additional penalty on her attack rolls up to her Wisdom bonus, and add the same amount as a circumstance bonus on her ‹rule/concentration› check. If the concentration check fails, the spell is wasted, but the attacks still take the penalty." ability="Also, when the ectoplasmatist casts a spiritualist spell with a range of touch, she can deliver the spell through one of her *ectoplasmic lashes.* Instead of making the free melee touch attack she would normally use to deliver the spell, the ectoplasmatist can choose to make a free *ectoplasmic lash* attack at her highest base attack bonus. A successful lash attack deals damage normally and imparts the spell's effects. The ectoplasmatist can't hold the charge if her lash attack misses." replace="Bonded manifestation~phantom recall~dual bond"}

::ab[Ectoplasmic Armor (Su)]{icon=def l4="When an ectoplasmatist manifests her *ectoplasmic lash* ability, tendrils of ectoplasmic material envelop her body, granting her a +4 armor bonus to AC." l12="The ectoplasmatist's armor bonus to AC increases to +6 and is treated as ‹magic-enh/ghost touch<_a› armor." replace="Spiritual interference~greater spiritual interference"}

exciter.name: Exciter
description: ## Exciter
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/68›

Psychic magic draws upon the twin and sometimes opposed powers of thought and emotion, but an exciter cares little for rationality. The phantom that accompanies him fills him with unbridled exultation, as he lets feeling and passion rule and sharpen his mind and body into a glorious fusion.

::ab[Merged Phantom]{icon=down ability="An exciter internalizes his phantom and merges it completely within his mind. He cannot fully manifest his phantom outside of his own body in incorporeal or ectoplasmic form. *Emotional focus* abilities that affect or require a manifested phantom are lost, except for any aura gained by the phantom at 7th level; if the phantom gains an aura at 7th level, this aura functions despite the phantom not being manifested, and is centered on the exciter." alter="Phantom~emotional focus" replace="6th-level phantom recall"}

::ab[Rapture (Su)]{icon=power-boost flavor="An exciter gains the ability to enter an ecstatic state in which he's consumed and overwhelmed by his passions and driven into a fighting fury." ability="This functions similarly to a ‹class/bloodrager«'s› *bloodrage,* treating his spiritualist level as his bloodrager level, though he doesn't qualify for feats or other elements that require *rage* or *bloodrage.* When entering a rapture, the exciter loses all other benefits from having his phantom confined in his consciousness (such as the ‹feat/Skill Focus› feats and bonus against mind-affecting effects), but he can choose to exchange the normal +4 morale bonus to his Strength and Constitution scores normally gained from *bloodrage* for a +4 morale bonus to his Dexterity and Charisma scores or a +2 morale bonus to his Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, and Charisma scores. In addition to the fatigue for ending a *bloodrage,* an exciter can't enter a rapture again for 1 minute after ending his last rapture." replace="Ability to manifest his phantom~ability to use *emotional focus* abilities that require a manifested phantom" head}

::ab[Bloodrage (Su)]{next icon=power-boost-def-down flavor="Text from the bloodrager class ability." order=ufp free="You can enter or leave a *bloodrage* as a free action." passive="While in a *bloodrage,* you gain a +4 morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution, as well as a +2 morale bonus on Will saves. In addition, you take a -2 penalty to Armor Class. The increase to Constitution grants you 2 hit points per Hit Die, but these disappear when the *bloodrage* ends and are not lost first like ‹rule/temporary hit points›. While *bloodraging,* you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except Acrobatics, Fly, Intimidate, and Ride) or any ability that requires patience or concentration." special="When the *bloodrage* ends, you are ‹misc/fatigued› for a number of rounds equal to twice the number of rounds spent in the *bloodrage.* You can't enter a new *bloodrage* while fatigued or ‹misc/exhausted›, but can otherwise enter *bloodrage* multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If you fall ‹misc/unconscious›, your *bloodrage* immediately ends, placing you in peril of death." usage="You can *bloodrage* for 2 + twice your level + your Con modifier rounds/day; these rounds need not be consecutive. Temporary increases to Con (such as those gained from *bloodraging* or spells like ‹spell/bear's endurance›) don't increase this number."}

::ab[Fast Movement (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="An exciter's land speed is faster than normal for his race by 10 feet. This benefit applies only when he is wearing light armor, medium armor, or no armor and is not carrying a heavy load. This doesn't stack with the ‹class/barbarian› ability of the same name." replace="Etheric tether"}

::ab[Excitation (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability="When an exciter is in a *rapture,* each round he can choose to intensify his emotional fury, expending 2 rounds of *rapture* instead of 1. When he uses *excitation,* he chooses one of the two abilities that his *emotional focus* would normally grant to a manifested phantom, such as *strength focus* and *powerful strike* for an ‹emotionalfocus/anger› phantom, or *power from despair* and *miserable strike* for a ‹emotionalfocus/despair› phantom. He gains these abilities until the beginning of his next turn." special="If the ability normally augments only a phantom's slam attacks, it instead applies to the ectoplasmic tendrils that the spiritualist can sprout using his ectoplasmic *bonded manifestation.*" replace="Bonded senses"}

::ab[Rapturous Rage]{l=10 icon=power ability="An exciter can select one ‹ability/rage power<s› for which he qualifies, treating his spiritualist level as his barbarian level for all purposes relating to that particular rage power (he still doesn't qualify for the ‹feat/Extra Rage Power› feat or any similar abilities)." repeat="He can choose another rage power, following the above restrictions.~14~4" replace="10th-, 14th-, and 18th-level phantom recall~spiritual bond"}

::ab[Perfect Passion (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="An exciter can cast psychic spells with an emotion component even while in his *rapture.* He can cast these spells defensively and attempt ‹rule/concentration› checks for these spells without impairment, despite being in a *rapture.* He also can attempt Charisma- and Intelligence-based skill checks related to psychic spells with an emotion component while in his *rapture.* He cannot cast arcane or divine spells or psychic spells that have a thought component while in a *rapture.*" replace="Spiritual interference"}

::ab[Overwhelming Excitment (Su)]{l=10 icon=power ability="An exciter can share the effects of his *rapture* with willing allies within 10 feet. He must expend 2 additional rounds of his *rapture* each round for each ally sharing its effects. The exciter's allies share all effects of the *rapture* except the rage powers; each ally must end its turn within 10 feet of the exciter, or the effects of the *rapture* end for that ally and it becomes fatigued." special="Whenever he is not sharing the effects of his *rapture* with an ally, the exciter now retains the benefits of his phantom in his consciousness even during *rapture.*" replace="Fused consciousness"}

::ab[Greater Rapture (Su)]{l=12 icon=boost-def ability="An exciter increases the morale bonus his *rapture* grants to each applicable ability score by 2 and the morale bonus he gains on Will saves by 1. In addition, upon entering a *rapture,* he can apply the effects of a single spiritualist spell he knows with an emotion component to himself. The spell must have a range of touch or personal, and it must be a 1st- or 2nd-level spell." incrementOrd="He can now apply~12~3~3" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd="-level spell with this ability." special="If he uses this ability to apply the effects of a spell again before the duration of the previous spell expires, the previous spell ends immediately." replace="Greater spiritual interference"}

fated_guide.name: Fated Guide
description: ## Fated Guide
‹SOURCE Occult Origins/18›

Not all phantoms are restless dead escaping the afterlife. On rare occasions, ‹faith/Pharasma› returns a judged soul to the Material Plane - linking the spirit to a devout believer - to complete some final task, make amends for a crime, or grant a sharply divided soul a second chance. Sadly, from a soul's perspective, the journey along the River of Souls and its trials in the Boneyard last eons, and most phantoms return with little, if any, idea of who they were or the nature of their dedicated purposes. The fated guide helps her phantom uncover details about its life and what it can do to finally move on.
::prereq{other="Phantom must have the ‹emotionalfocus/remorse› emotional focus"}
::ab[Shepherd to the Dead]{icon=learn info="A fated guide adds ‹spell/disrupt undead› and ‹spell/sanctify corpse› to her list of spells known."}

::ab[Divine Purpose]{icon=power ability="A fated guide's phantom gains ‹feat/Deific Obedience› as a bonus feat, though the spiritualist must obey her god's obedience to gain any benefit." replace="Phantom's antagonistic ability"}

::ab[Thanatopic Bonded Manifestation (Su)]{icon=power-magic-def-touch flavor="A fated guide can bond with her phantom to channel the influence of Pharasma and her servitors." l=3 order=ca choice="When a spiritualist gains this ability, she must replace her ability to form either an ectoplasmic *bonded manifestation* or an incorporeal *bonded manifestation.*" ability="When the fated guide uses this *thanatopic bond,* she gains access to the benefits of her phantom's Deific Obedience feat, and can use ‹spell/speak with dead› as per the spell, asking a maximum of one question per spiritualist level she has each day, and no more than one question per corpse; asking a question and getting an answer requires 2 full rounds." imp8="The fated guide can act normally even after being reduced below 0 hit points. The spiritualist immediately dies if reduced to a number of negative hit points equal to her Constitution score. She takes 1 extra point of damage each round she performs a strenuous action, such as attacking or casting a spell, and immediately falls unconscious and begins dying if she dismisses the *thanatopic bond* or the duration of *bonded manifestation* expires. Maintaining her nearly dead form is taxing, and each round that she remains active below 0 hit points consumes 2 rounds of bonded manifestation's duration." imp13="While using her *thanatopic bonded manifestation,* the fated guide can cast ‹spell/antilife shell› as a spell-like ability. Maintaining this spell quickly exhausts her phantom, and each round she maintains the spell consumes 2 rounds of bonded manifestation's duration." imp18="The fated guide's touch banishes the unquiet dead. She can deal 10d6 points of positive energy damage (Will half; DC = 10 + 1/2 the fated guide's spiritualist level + her Wisdom modifier) as a touch attack against undead creatures. An undead creature that successfully saves against this touch attack is immune to the same fated guide's positive energy touch for 24 hours." alter="Bonded manifestation"}
::ab[Iron Bond (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="Her phantom gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against ‹spell/banishment›, ‹spell/dismissal›, or other similar effects." replace="See invisibility"}

::ab[Undeniable Bond (Ex)]{l=16 icon=def passive="Her phantom gains an additional +4 bonus on saving throws against *banishment, dismissal,* or other similar effects, for a total of +8." replace="Call spirit"}

fractured_mind.name: Fractured Mind
description: ## Fractured Mind
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/109›

Most spiritualists harbor the spirits of the deceased in their psyches, but a small number of them - known as fractured minds - draw their powers instead from a fraction of their own souls that resonates with extremely powerful emotions. These spiritualists' phantoms are not spiritual allies, but rather extensions of the fractured minds' own inner thoughts and emotions.

::ab[Emotional Spellcasting]{icon=info info="A fractured mind's ability to cast spells is tied to the force of her own spirit rather than her connection to the spirit world. As a result, she uses her Charisma score rather than her Wisdom score to determine the highest spell level she can cast, the saving throw DCs of spells she casts, and her bonus spells per day." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Emotional Power (Sp)]{head icon=magic ability="The fractured mind gains a number of spell-like abilities, which are tied to her phantom's emotional focus. She gains one spell-like ability at 5th level, a second at 7th level, a third at 9th level, and a fourth at 16th level. A fractured mind can use each of these abilities once per day, plus one additional time per day for every 4 spiritualist levels she possesses beyond the level at which she gained the spell-like ability. The saving throw DCs for these spell-like abilities are equal to 10 + 1/2 the fractured mind's spiritualist class level + the fractured mind's Charisma modifier, rather than being based on the spell's level. The spell-like abilities granted by each emotional focus are listed below." replace="Detect undead~calm spirit~see invisibility~call spirit" imp9="She can use the 5th-level power 2 times a day." incrementMulti="She can use the 5th-level power ~ times a day and the 9th-level power ~ times a day.~9/4;3/1;2/1~~She can use the 7th-level power ~ times a day.~7/4;2/1" imp20="She can use the 16th-level power 2 times a day."}
::ab[Anger]{next icon=magic s5="Enlarge person" s7=Rage s9=Longstrider s16=Shout}
::ab[Dedication]{next icon=magic s5=Command s7="Spiritual weapon" s9=Bless s16="Death ward"}
::ab[Despair]{next icon=magic s5=Bungle s7="Stricken heart" s9=Doom s16="Crushing despair"}
::ab[Fear]{next icon=magic s5=Bane s7="Mad hallucination" s9=Doom s16="Phantasmal killer"}
::ab[Hatred]{next icon=magic s5=Doom s7="Inflict moderate wounds" s9=Bane s16="Bestow curse"}
::ab[Jealousy]{next icon=magic s5=Sanctuary s7="Resist energy" s9="Entropic shield" s16="Spell immunity"}
::ab{title="Kindness@FN1" next icon=magic s5=Bless s7="Calm emotions" s9=Heroism s16="Good hope"}
::ab{title="Remorse@FN2" next icon=magic s5=Doom s7="Inflict pain" s9="Stricken heart" s16="Mind fog"}
::ab{title="Whimsey@FN3" next icon=magic s5=Ventriloquism s7=Absurdity s9="Major image" s16="Rainbow pattern"}
::ab[Zeal]{next icon=magic s5=Bless s7="Spiritual weapon" s9="Divine favor" s16="Freedom of movement"}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[‹source/Psychic Anthology› pg. 4]{from=1}
@FN[‹source/Occult Origins› pg. 19]{from=2}
@FN[‹source/Heroes from the Fringe› pg. 17]{from=3}

geist_channeler.name: Geist Channeler
description: ## Geist Channeler
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/109›

Some phantoms retain less of their personalities and memories than others. Though most phantoms maintain some semblance of their former selves, others of their kind, known as ‹monster/geist«s›, came close to being lost forever before they became phantoms. Incapable of manifesting in ectoplasmic form and devoid of human emotions, these strange entities are much closer to ghosts than most other phantoms. The spiritualists who work with them, known as geist channelers, are able to call on them in unique ways.

::ab[Geistform Phantom (Su)]{icon=down-boost order=na info="A geist channeler's phantom can't manifest in ectoplasmic form, and is limited to manifesting in incorporeal form. As a result, the phantom has no Strength score, and has a starting Wisdom score of 12. Instead of gaining two slam attacks, it gains only a single slam attack that deals 1d8 points of damage (for a Medium phantom; perform ‹misc/damage die adjustments› for differently-sized phantoms)." imp4="The phantom does not gain the *magic attacks* ability at 4th level." ability="While manifested incorporeally, a geist channeler's phantom can attack only other incorporeal creatures. Because the phantom is incorporeal, the damage it deals to such creatures is not reduced as a result of them being incorporeal." imp5="The slam attack's damage increases to 1d10 points (Medium). The phantom can take a @HLswift_action to infuse itself with trace amounts of ectoplasm so it can attack corporeal creatures for 1 round. During that time, it deals half damage to corporeal creatures with its slam, and corporeal creatures with nonmagical weapons deal half damage to the phantom." imp10="The slam attack's damage increases to 2d6 (Medium). When the phantom infuses itself, its attacks deal normal damage to corporeal creatures." imp15="The slam attack's damage increases to 2d8 (Medium)." imp20="The slam attack's damage increases to 2d10 (Medium)" alter=Phantom}

::ab[Spiritual Manifestation (Su)]{icon=down ability="Because of the nature of her phantom, a geist channeler can use the *bonded manifestation* ability to manifest his phantom only in incorporeal form." alter="Bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Emotionless (Su)]{head icon=down-boost-power order=na info="A geist channeler's phantom doesn't have an emotional focus; instead, it is cold and devoid of emotions." ability="The phantom gains a number of ranks in Knowledge (arcana) and Knowledge (religion) equal to its Hit Dice. While confined in the geist channeler's consciousness, the phantom grants the geist channeler ‹feat/Skill Focus› in each of these skills." special="The phantom has ‹misc/good« Reflex and Will» saves›, and gains ‹feat/Iron Will› as a bonus feat, as well as the following abilities."}

::ab[Unnerving Touch (Su)]{next icon=touch standard="A geist channeler's phantom can make a melee touch attack against a living creature, even if that creature is corporeal. If the attack hits, the target is ‹misc/shaken› for 1d4+1 rounds. A successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the phantom's Hit Dice + the phantom's Charisma modifier) negates this effect." imp5="When the phantom can damage corporeal creatures with its slam attack, its slam attacks against living corporeal creatures gain this ability." special="Multiple attacks against the same creature don't cause the creature to become ‹misc/frightened›. This is a mind-affecting fear effect."}

::ab[Poltergeist's Knack (Sp)]{next l=7 icon=magic useInc=phantom~3~7~3 useHD ability="A geist channeler's phantom can use ‹spell/telekinesis› as a spell-like ability. It can use only the *sustained force* and *violent thrust* versions of this spell. Its caster level for this spell-like ability is equal to the phantom's Hit Dice."}

::ab[Spectral Protection (Su)]{next l=12 icon=aura standard="While a geist channeler's phantom is confined within his psyche, he can call upon its power to project an aura of spectral energy that prevents incorporeal creatures from approaching him. This takes the form of a 30-foot-radius ‹misc/emanation› centered on the geist channeler. Any incorporeal creature attempting to enter this area must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the geist channeler's spiritualist class level + the geist channeler's Wisdom modifier) or be unable to do so. This ability can be used only defensively, not aggressively; forcing the aura against an incorporeal creature collapses the aura." useL=spiritualist useUnit=minute useNC=1}

::ab[Malevolent Possession (Sp)]{next l=17 icon=magic ability="Once per day, a geist channeler's phantom can invade the bodies of other creatures. This functions as ‹spell/possession›. To use this ability, the phantom must be adjacent to the target. The target can resist the effect with a successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the phantom's Hit Dice + the phantom's Charisma modifier)." alter=Phantom replace="The phantom's emotional focus"}

grim_apostle.name: Grim Apostle
description: ## Grim Apostle
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #144: Midwives to Death/72›
Some devotees of the Four Horsemen can form spiritual manifestations of the daemonic demigods.

::ab[Grim Phantom (Su)]{icon=info-down order=a2 ability="The spirit linked to a grim apostle functions in all respects as a regular spiritualist's phantom, but each day when the grim apostle regains her daily spell slots, she can choose which Horseman focus (see below) is dominant within the grim phantom." ability2="When the grim phantom is banished to the Ethereal Plane, the grim apostle takes an amount of damage equal to 1d4 &times& the grim phantom's number of Hit Dice." alter=Phantom replace="The phantom's emotional focus"}

::ab[Hardened Soul (Su)]{l=1 icon=def passive="While a grim phantom is confined in the grim apostle's consciousness, the grim apostle gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against death effects and effects that would remove or subsume the grim apostle's soul (such as ‹spell/possession› or ‹spell/trap the soul›)." increment="This bonus~0~6~6~2" replace="Shared consciousness~fused consciousness"}
### Horseman Focus
Instead of an emotional focus, a grim phantom has one of the following Horseman foci.

The DC for a saving throw against any of the grim phantom's abilities is equal to 10 + 1/2 the grim phantom's Hit Dice + the grim phantom's Charisma modifier. For any spell-like abilities a grim phantom receives, it uses its Hit Dice as its caster level.
::h4[Death]{jl}
Grim phantoms influenced by ‹faith/Charon›, the Horseman of Death, are dark, almost shapeless specters.
::ab[Skills and Saves]{icon=boost-def ability="The grim phantom gains ranks in Knowledge (history) and Stealth equal to its number of Hit Dice. Its good saves are Fortitude and Will."}
::ab[Fleet]{icon=power ability="The grim phantom gains ‹feat/Fleet› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Marked for Death (Su)]{icon=boost ability="When manifested in ectoplasmic form, the grim phantom gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls against a creature the grim apostle has hit with a melee or ranged attack since its last turn (the phantom's &quot&marked target&quot&). Additionally, such an attack by the grim phantom deals additional **X**d6 negative energy damage, where **X** is 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of his spiritualist level."}

::ab[Fatal Aura (Su)]{l=7 icon=aura-lower swift="The grim phantom can create a 10-foot-radius aura that makes healing more difficult. The DC of Heal checks to provide first aid and treat deadly wounds within the aura is increased by 5. Any creature within the aura that casts a conjuration (healing) spell, or casts another spell or uses an ability that restores hit points, must succeed at a Will saving throw or lose the spell or use of the ability. Ending the aura is a @HLfree_action."}

::ab[No Escape (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="The grim phantom gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Following Step›, ‹feat/Step Up›, and ‹feat/Step Up and Strike› feats but only against its *marked target* (see above)."}
::ab[Killing Word (Sp)]{l=17 icon=magic ability="The grim phantom can cast ‹spell/power word kill› once per day as a spell-like ability."}
::h4[Famine]{jl}
Grim phantoms influenced by ‹faith/Trelmarixian›, the Horseman of Famine, are gaunt with jackal-like features.
::ab[Skills and Saves]{icon=boost-def ability="The grim phantom gains ranks in Perception and Sense Motive equal to its number of Hit Dice. Its good saves are Reflex and Will."}
::ab[Weapon Finesse]{icon=power ability="The grim phantom gains ‹feat/Weapon Finesse› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Ravenous Bite (Ex)]{icon=boost-power ability="The grim phantom's natural attacks take the form of bites that deal piercing damage as a creature ‹misc/one size category larger› than its current size. It also gains ‹feat/Power Attack› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Aura of Starvation (Su)]{l=7 icon=aura-lower swift="The grim phantom can create a 10-foot-radius *aura of starvation.* Enemies within the aura take a -2 penalty on attack and damage rolls. Ending the aura is a @HLfree_action."}
::ab[Wail of the Hungry (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic ability="The grim phantom can cast ‹spell/waves of fatigue› once per day as a spell-like ability."}

::ab[Distended Gullet (Su)]{l=17 icon=melee swift="The grim phantom in ectoplasmic form gains the ‹umr/swallow whole› ability (4d8 negative energy damage, AC 17, one-tenth the phantom's total hit points) for a number of rounds equal to its Charisma modifier. This ability can affect creatures no more than two size categories larger than the phantom. Each creature the grim phantom swallows is shunted into an extradimensional space that can hold any number of creatures. When the duration of this ability ends, any creatures still within the extradimensional space appear adjacent to the grim phantom." special="The grim phantom is ‹misc/exhausted› for 1 hour after using this ability and can't use it again during that time."}
::h4[Pestilence]{jl}
Grim phantoms influenced by ‹faith/Apollyon›, the Horseman of Pestilence, often have rams' skulls for heads.
::ab[Skills and Saves]{icon=boost-def ability="The grim phantom gains ranks in Acrobatics and Heal equal to its number of Hit Dice. Its good saves are Fortitude and Will."}
::ab[Great Fortitude]{icon=power ability="The grim phantom gains ‹feat/Great Fortitude› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Weakening Strike (Su)]{icon=down ability="A creature hit by the grim phantom's slam attack must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw or take a -2 penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks for 1d4 rounds." special="This is a disease effect. Penalties from multiple hits don't stack."}

::ab[Aura of Flies (Su)]{l=7 icon=aura swift="The grim phantom can surround itself with a cloud of biting flies. Any creature that ends its turn adjacent to the grim phantom takes 1d6 piercing damage. Ending the aura is a @HLfree_action." incrementPlain="This damage~7~4" incrementEnd="d6."}
::ab[Contagious Touch (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic ability="The grim phantom can cast ‹spell/contagion› three times per day as a spell-like ability."}

::ab[Virulent Attack (Su)]{l=17 icon=zap-lower passive="Any creature under the effects of the grim phantom's *weakening strike* becomes a dangerous vector for disease. Any creature that ends its turn adjacent to the target creature is immediately exposed to any diseases the target creature has, no matter the normal method of contracting that disease. The creature must immediately attempt a saving throw against that disease, and the onset period doesn't apply."}
::h4[War]{jl}
Grim phantoms influenced by ‹faith/Szuriel›, the Horseman of War, look like sinister, black-winged angels.
::ab[Skills and Saves]{icon=boost-def ability="The grim phantom gains ranks in Intimidate and Survival equal to its number of Hit Dice. Its good saves are Fortitude and Reflex."}

::ab[Strength Focus]{icon=boost-down info="The grim phantom gains a +2 bonus to Strength and a -2 penalty to Dexterity. The phantom gains bonuses to Strength instead of Dexterity as the grim apostle gains spiritualist levels."}

::ab[Ruthless Combatant (Su)]{icon=boost ability="The grim phantom threatens a critical hit with its slam attack on a roll of 19-20." imp11="The phantom's critical modifier with slam attacks increases to &times&3. This doesn't stack with ‹feat/Improved Critical› or similar effects."}

::ab[Aura of Fury (Su)]{l=7 icon=aura-boost-lower swift="The grim phantom can create a 20-foot-radius aura. Creatures within the aura gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls but take a -2 penalty to AC. Ending the aura is a @HLfree_action."}
::ab[Bloodlust (Su)]{l=12 icon=aid ability="The grim phantom regains 1d6 hit points with each successful slam attack it makes."}

::ab[Juggernaut (Su)]{l=17 icon=def passive="The grim phantom becomes immune to any effects that would cause it to become cowering, exhausted, fatigued, frightened, panicked, shaken, or slowed (as the ‹spell/slow› spell)."}

hag_haunted.name: Hag-Haunted
description: ## Hag-Haunted
‹SOURCE Blood of the Coven/23›

‹family/Hag«s› - those of flesh and blood, at any rate - die like any other mortals, and their souls normally depart to the Outer Planes for judgment. A hag who dies with a curse on her breath is often anchored to the Ethereal Plane by the power of her hatred - similar to vile and angry mortal souls - and some even claw their way back to the living world through the souls of those they despised or ruined... or those unfortunate souls they birthed. Hag-haunted spiritualists are tethered to these spiteful spirits, anchoring them once again in the world of the living. While this partnership imparts powerful magic, they run the constant risk of serving as little more than mounts for their overwhelming phantoms.

Hag-haunted spiritualists are rarely the masters in their relationship with their phantoms, and the only tool at their disposal to control their wicked minion is to dismiss them back to the Ethereal Plane. In the best scenarios, the relationship is one of mutual competition and constant bargaining, but just as often the hag phantom dominates and abuses her spiritualist.

::ab[Hag Phantom]{icon=info-power order=npa info="A hag phantom forms from the soul of a deceased hag. She always has an evil alignment, rather than matching the spiritualist's alignment, and must select one of the following emotional focuses: ‹emotionalfocus/anger›, ‹emotionalfocus/hatred›, ‹emotionalfocus/jealousy›, or ‹emotionalfocus/zeal›." passive="The hag phantom starts with a +2 bonus to Strength and Intelligence and has her own agenda - usually contrary to the spiritualist's - though she recognizes that the spiritualist can unmanifest her, and therefore she typically hides suspicious actions from her spiritualist." ability="When in the spiritualist's consciousness, the hag phantom can grant the hag-haunted ‹feat/Spell Focus› (necromancy) and ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Bluff), but she often revokes them if the spiritualist banishes her there as a punishment, and she might use them as leverage to get what she wants." imp10="The hag can also grant the Skill Focus feats while manifested." imp12="The hag can also grant ‹feat/Greater Spell Focus› (necromancy)." alter=Phantom replace="Shared consciousness~fused consciousness"}

::ab[Hag Spellcasting]{flavor="A hag-haunted spiritualist's spells come from her connection to her hag phantom." icon=info info="Her spells are considered arcane rather than psychic, and they use verbal and somatic components instead of thought and emotion components. She still selects her spells known from the spiritualist class list."}

::ab[Death Curse (Sp)]{icon=magic-learn flavor="A hag phantom is partially animated by the power of a hag's dying curse, and this eventually grants her a death curse of her own." l=4 immediate="Whenever the hag phantom is slain and returned to the Ethereal Plane, the phantom can utter a curse on the creature that killed her, targeting it with ‹spell/bestow curse›." imp6="The spiritualist gains *bestow curse* as an extra 3rd-level spell known." imp16="The spiritualist gains ‹spell/major curse› as an additional 6th-level spell known." replace="Spiritual interference~greater spiritual interference"}

haunted.name: Haunted
description: ## Haunted
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/110›
The haunted are a dangerous breed of spellcasters bound to temperamental and unpredictable phantoms that leech power from their spiritualists.

::ab[Usurp Manifestation (Su)]{l=3 icon=down-boost-def order=nui info="The haunted's phantom begins to subtly subvert the relationship with its master, gaining rather than granting power from the connection. When fully manifested in ectoplasmic form, the phantom can use its *usurp manifestation* ability to draw upon the haunted's body to strengthen its ectoplasmic might, temporarily weakening the spiritualist in order to increase its own power." useL=spiritualist3 useNC immediate="The phantom must activate this ability at the start of the spiritualist's turn. The phantom can dismiss the effects of a *usurped manifestation* as a @HLfree_action on its turn. While this ability is in effect, the haunted's features become sunken and withdrawn, and she gains the ‹misc/nauseated› condition until the effect ends. Conversely, the phantom surges in size and power, becoming one size category larger (as if affected by an ‹spell/enlarge person› spell) and increasing its natural armor bonus to AC by 2 for the effect's duration." special="If the spiritualist prevents or mitigates the nauseated condition, the phantom is unable to siphon energy and doesn't gain the benefits of *usurp manifestation.*"}

::ab[Usurp Spell]{l=8 icon=magic flavor="While using *usurp manifestation,* the phantom can leech spell slots from the haunted to increase its own power." swift="The phantom can reach through the etheric tether to drain the nauseated haunted's psychic spell energy, effectively taking any touch spell or spell with a target of &quot&you&quot& that the haunted knows and casting it on itself (assuming the haunted has not yet used up her allotment of spells per day for the spell's level)." special="This ability consumes two of the haunted's spell slots of the appropriate level and otherwise follows the rules for the *share spells* and *deliver touch spells* abilities."}
::ab[Teleplasmic Surge]{l=13 icon=boost passive="When using *usurp manifestation,* the phantom gains a +2 morale bonus on attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks."}

::ab[Usurped Bond (Su)]{l=17 icon=info info="The haunted's phantom can use *usurp manifestation* for a number of rounds per day equal to 3 + double the haunted's spiritualist level." replace="Dual bond"}

::ab[Ferocious Phantom]{l=18 icon=def passive="When using *usurp manifestation* the phantom gains DR 10/slashing and spell resistance equal to 11 + the haunted's spiritualist level (this spell resistance doesn't apply to spells originating from the spiritualist or from the *usurp spell* ability)." replace="Bonded manifestation"}

involutionist.name: Involutionist
description: ## Involutionist
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/167›

The ‹rule/Rivethun› teach their followers to cultivate a well of inner power and to open themselves to the spiritual world, but on occasion - especially among neophytes who join the cult later in life - these processes overlap, and the acolyte invests her spiritual reservoir into some aspect of the world around her. Rather than bond with an existing spirit as most Rivethun eventually do, the involutionist creates a spirit from a piece of her own soul. The bond is deep, and it creates a powerful minion utterly loyal to her creator, but it also cuts off the involutionist's access to the wider spiritual world.

::ab[Divine Spellcasting]{icon=magic info="An involutionist is a divine spellcaster. Her spells use verbal components instead of thought components and somatic components instead of emotional components. An involutionist's spirit phantom is her conduit to the divine, and it serves as her divine focus; the involutionist must either be within 5 feet on her spirit phantom or absorb her phantom into her consciousness to cast spells that require a divine focus." alter=Spellcasting}

:::ab{flavor="An involutionist's phantom is manifested from her own psyche and invested into the spirit-stuff of the world around her." icon=magic action=Ability title="Spirit Phantom" repeat="The spirit phantom gains one additional hex from their list.~7~5" alter=Phantom replace="Phantom's emotional focus"}

Rather than an emotional focus, the involutionist selects a spirit from those available to the ‹class/shaman›. A spirit phantom always has ‹misc/good« Fortitude and Reflex» saves›, and it gains a number of ranks equal to the phantom's Hit Dice in two skills depending on the spirit it represents, as summarized on the table below.
| Spirit | Associated Skills |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Battle] | Intimidate and Sense Motive |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Bones] | Heal and Stealth |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Flame] | Acrobatics and Intimidate |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Heavens] | Acrobatics and Fly |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Life] | Diplomacy and Heal |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Lore] | Knowledge (arcana) and Linguistics |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Nature] | Knowledge (nature) and Survival |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Stone] | Appraise and Knowledge (engineering) |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Waves] | Diplomacy and Swim |
| @ripple[shamanspirit/Wind] | Bluff and Sense Motive |

When fully manifested, the involutionist's spirit phantom gains the special ability normally applied to a shaman's spirit animal. The spirit phantom also gains a single hex from the list of those provided by that spirit.

::ab[Spirit Manifestation (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="When an involutionist uses her *bonded manifestation* ability, she can instead manifest her spirit phantom's spirit animal special ability and use her spirit phantom's hexes as if they were her own. Hexes used during this manifestation count toward any limited number of uses per day." alter="Bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Spirit Awareness (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic flavor="An involutionist gains great insight into the spiritual influence people invest into the world around them." ability="She can cast ‹spell/detect psychic significance› as a spell-like ability at will and ‹spell/analyze aura› once per day as a spell-like ability (CL = her character level)." replace="Detect undead"}

::ab[Involuate (Sp)]{l=11 icon=magic flavor="An involutionist gains the ability to awaken the latent spiritual energy in inanimate objects." ability="She gains the ability to cast ‹spell/animate objects› as a spell-like ability (CL = her character level)." useInc=spiritualist~4~11 useF="11~1~4" replace="Additional uses of calm spirit available at 11th, 15th, and 19th levels"}

necrologist.name: Necrologist
description: ## Necrologist
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/69›

All spiritualists call upon residual phantoms that cling to the living world through force of will and unresolved passion. The vile spiritualists known as necrologists reach farther toward the Negative Energy Plane, however, and for whatever evil purpose, they bring back malevolent spirits whose passions have turned to vengeance and hate for all life.
::prereq{other="Evil alignment"}

::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning info="If a necrologist becomes nonevil, she can continue to take spiritualist levels, but her phantom ceases to give her benefits while in her consciousness, she can't use *bonded manifestation,* and if she manifests the phantom, it is no longer loyal to her and likely attacks her and her allies." alter=Alignment}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn info="A necrologist adds the following spells to her class list at the listed spell levels." s1="Hide from undead" s2="Ghostly disguise~haunting mists" s3="Haunting choir" s4="Lesser age resistance" s5="Age resistance" s6="Greater age resistance~create greater undead"}
::ab[Bonded Manifestation]{icon=down info="A necrologist cannot use ectoplasmic bonded manifestation." alter="Bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Shared Conciousness]{icon=def passive="A necrologist gains a bonus on saving throws against death effects, energy drain, and negative energy rather than against mind-affecting effects (and can shunt such an effect onto the phantom) when her *undead phantom* is contained within her consciousness." alter="Shared consciousness"}

::ab[Undead Phantom]{icon=info info="A necrologist's phantom is undead, rather than an outsider. It has normal undead immunities and no Constitution score; uses d8 Hit Dice, each of which is modified by the phantom's Charisma modifier as normal; and gains the abilities below at the levels indicated. An undead phantom otherwise follows the standard phantom rules (for instance, its base attack bonus and skills don't change to match undead)."}

::ab[Cling of the Grave (Su)]{icon=magic ability="An undead phantom can ‹rule/hold the charge› on a touch spell of the necromancy school up to a maximum of 1 round per spiritualist level of the necrologist." alter="Share spells"}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{l=4 icon=aura useMod=Charisma1 ability="A necrologist gains the ability to ‹ability/channel negative energy›, with an effective cleric level equal to her spiritualist level - 3." replace="Spiritual interference"}

::ab[Unnatural Aura (Su)]{l=5 icon=def passive="Animals do not willingly approach within 30 feet of a fully manifested undead phantom unless the necrologist succeeds at a Handle Animal, Ride, or wild empathy check (DC = 10 + 1/2 the necrologist's spiritualist level + the phantom's Charisma modifier)." replace="Slam damage increase gained at 5th level"}
::ab[Channel Resistance (Su)]{l=6 icon=def passive="A necrologist's phantom gains a +4 bonus on saving throws to resist the effects of *channel energy.*" replace=Devotion}

::ab[Necropsychic Conduit]{l=12 icon=power ability="A necrologist can cast psychic spells as if she had the ‹feat/Thanatopic Spell› and ‹feat/Threnodic Spell› metamagic feats, using her undead phantom as a conduit to pierce the defenses of undead creatures." special="If her phantom has been banished or is not within 30 feet of her when she casts the spells, however, these feats have no effect." replace="Greater spiritual interference"}

::ab[Lifedrinker (Su)]{l=13 icon=magic ability="When a necrologist's phantom reduces a creature to fewer than 0 hit points with an attack or spell, the necrologist can expend one use of her *channel energy* ability as a @HLswift_action to affect the same target with ‹spell/death knell›. If the target fails its save, the necrologist can choose either herself or her phantom to gain the benefit of the spell." replace="Slam damage increase gained at 13th level"}

onmyoji.name: Onmyoji
description: ## Onmyoji
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/111›

Though most spiritualists are chosen by their phantoms, others deliberately call phantoms to them through years of careful preparation and study in obscure divine traditions. These spiritualists, known as onmyoji, form close bonds with their phantoms, as any other spiritualist does, but see the phantoms as partners and tools in their work. Onmyoji serve as emissaries between the mundane world and the spiritual one, either working to ensure that troubles in the spiritual world do not spill over into the world of mortals, or stirring up spiritual trouble in order to achieve their ends among the living.

::ab[Divine Spellcasting]{icon=magic info="An onmyoji's spellcasting ability comes from divine rather than psychic power. As a divine caster, the onmyoji's spells use verbal components instead of thought components, and somatic components instead of emotional components, and she uses an ofuda as a divine focus. Ofudas are scrolls with holy writings written on parchment, cloth, or wood (having the same cost as a ‹eq-misc/wooden holy symbol›) or metal (having the same cost as a ‹eq-misc/silver holy symbol›)." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Spiritual Resistance (Su)]{flavor="An onmyoji's phantom provides her with protection from supernatural forces." icon=def passive="While the phantom is confined within the onmyoji's consciousness (not stranded on the Ethereal Plane or fully manifested), it grants her a +2 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of fey, outsiders, and incorporeal undead." increment="This bonus~0~6~3" replace="Shared consciousness~fused consciousness"}

::ab[Divine Teachings (Su)]{flavor="An onmyoji gains the ability to call upon her phantom to help her cast spells she normally couldn't." icon=learn l4="An onmyoji can choose a single spell from the ‹main/cleric spell« list› with a spell level she is currently able to cast, and add that spell to her list of spells known, at the same spell level as it appears on the cleric spell list." repeat="She selects another cleric spell, following the restrictions above.~7~3" special="Because the onmyoji relies on her phantom's influence in order to cast these spells, she can cast spells added in this way only while the phantom is confined within her consciousness (not stranded on the Ethereal Plane or fully manifested)." replace="Spiritual interference~greater spiritual interference"}

phantom_blade.name: Phantom Blade
description: ## Phantom Blade
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/4›

A small selection of spiritualists are connected to spirits of combat and warfare, and manifest a weapon instead of a phantom. These spiritualists are known as phantom blades and fight with ectoplasmic weapons. Phantom blades are often found in Mendev and the Worldwound, where spiritualists are baptized in the blood of comrades; the last survivor of an unfortunate squad might return bearing one of these ghostly blades.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power info="A phantom blade is proficient with all simple and martial weapons. She is also proficient with light and medium armor." alter="Weapon~armor proficiency"}

::ab[Phantom Weapon]{icon=power ability="A phantom blade begins play with an ectoplasmic sentient weapon known as a *phantom weapon* whose weapon type is chosen by the phantom blade. The weapon type must be one with which the phantom blade is proficient. A *phantom weapon* functions similarly to the *black blade* of the ‹arc-magus/bladebound› magus archetype except as noted on the Phantom Weapon Progression table below. A phantom blade with this class feature can't have a phantom of any kind, even from another class." replace="Phantom~phantom recall~spiritual bond~dual bond"}

::ab[Spell Combat (Ex)]{head l=1 icon=power ability="A phantom blade can cast spells and wield her *phantom weapon* at the same time. This functions as the ‹class/magus«'s› *spell combat* class ability." replace="Etheric tether~shared consciousness~fused consciousness~empowered consciousness"}

::ab[Spell Combat (Ex)]{next flavor="Text from the magus class ability." order=nr info="A magus learn to cast spells and wield his weapons at the same time. This functions much like ‹rule/two-weapon fighting›, but the off-hand weapon is a spell that is being cast." icon=power-melee-magic special="To use this ability, you must have one hand free (even if the spell being cast does not have somatic components), while wielding a light or one-handed melee weapon in the other hand." fullround="You can make all of your attacks with your melee weapon at a -2 penalty and can also cast any spell from the magus spell list with a casting time of 1 standard action (any attack roll made as part of this spell also takes this penalty). If you cast this spell defensively, you can decide to take an additional penalty on your attack rolls, up to your Intelligence bonus, and add the same amount as a circumstance bonus on your ‹rule/concentration› check. If the check fails, the spell is wasted, but the attacks still take the penalty. You can choose to cast the spell first or make the weapon attacks first, but if you have more than one attack, you cannot cast the spell between weapon attacks."}

::ab[Spellstrike (Su)]{l=2 head icon=melee ability="A phantom blade can deliver spells through her *phantom weapon.* This functions like the magus's *spellstrike* class ability except the only weapon the spiritualist can use to deliver spells is her *phantom weapon.*" replace="Bonded senses"}

::ab[Spellstrike (Su)]{flavor="Text from the magus class ability." next icon=melee ability="Whenever you cast a spell with a range of &quot&touch&quot& from the magus spell list, you can deliver the spell through any weapon you are wielding as part of a melee attack. Instead of the free melee touch attack normally allowed to deliver the spell, you can make one free melee attack with your weapon (at your highest base attack bonus) as part of casting this spell. If successful, this melee attack deals its normal damage as well as the effects of the spell. If you make this attack in concert with *spell combat,* this melee attack takes all the penalties accrued by spell combat melee attacks." special="This attack uses the weapon's critical range (20, 19-20, or 18-20 and modified by the *keen* weapon property or similar effects), but the spell effect only deals &times&2 damage on a successful critical hit, while the weapon damage uses its own critical modifier."}

::ab[Spirit of War (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A phantom blade gains a bonus combat feat. The spiritualist must meet the prerequisites for these feats, but she treats her spiritualist level as her base attack bonus (in addition to base attack bonuses gained from other classes and racial Hit Dice) for the purpose of qualifying for these feats. The phantom blade also treats her spiritualist level as her fighter level for the purpose of selecting feats with a minimum number of fighter levels as a prerequisite." repeat="She gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above.~8~5" replace="Bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Etheric Focus (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="The phantom blade can use her *phantom weapon* to aid her in casting spells with ‹rule/thought »components<_oa›." swift="She can center herself (instead of as a move action)." imp12="The spiritualist can center herself as a @HLfree_action." replace="Spiritual interference~greater spiritual interference"}
::h3[Phantom Weapon Ability Descriptions]{jl}

A *phantom weapon* has special abilities (or imparts abilities to its wielder) depending on the wielder's spiritualist level. These abilities are cumulative. A *phantom weapon* normally refuses to use any of its abilities when wielded by anyone other than its phantom blade, and acts as an incorporeal masterwork weapon of its type.
::h3[Phantom Weapon Progression]{jl}
| Spiritualist Class Level | Enhancement Bonus | Cha | Int/Wis | Ego | Special |
| 1st-2nd | +0 | 10 | 6 | 5 | Alertness, Phantom Touch, telepathy, weapon of the mind |
| 3rd-4th | +1 | 11 | 7 | 5 | Quick manifest |
| 5th-6th | +2 | 12 | 8 | 8 | Ectoplasmic pool |
| 9th-10th | +3 | 14 | 10 | 12 | Phantom tether |
| 13th-14th | +4 | 16 | 12 | 16 | Quick Reshape |
| 17th-18th | +5 | 18 | 14 | 22 | Etheric strikes |
::ab[Alertness (Ex)]{icon=power ability="While the phantom blade is wielding or harboring her *phantom weapon,* she gains the ‹feat/Alertness› feat."}

::ab[Phantom Touch (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A *phantom weapon* is always treated as a ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› weapon, regardless of weapon type or any other abilities modifying the weapon. If the *phantom weapon* would ever be destroyed, it is instead harbored in the spiritualist's consciousness for 24 hours."}
::ab[Telepathy (Su)]{icon=magic ability="While the phantom blade is wielding or carrying her *phantom weapon,* she can communicate telepathically with the weapon."}

::ab[Weapon of the Mind (Ex)]{icon=power ability="The phantom blade can harbor her *phantom weapon* in her consciousness or manifest it in its ectoplasmic form. The phantom blade can manifest the weapon through a ritual that takes 1 minute to perform. Harboring the *phantom weapon* requires a @HLfull-round_action. While the weapon is harbored, the phantom blade gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat and treats her unarmed strike damage as a ‹class/monk› of her level - 2. If the phantom blade already has this feat, she instead gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (unarmed strike)." special="The phantom blade's unarmed strikes gain the *phantom weapon's* enhancement bonus and other abilities when the weapon is harbored. The *phantom weapon* also recovers a number of hit points equal to its ego score every hour that it is harbored."}
::ab[Quick Manifest (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="The phantom blade can manifest her *phantom weapon* as a @HLswift_action."}

::ab[Ectoplasmic Pool (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost order=a2w ability="The phantom blade gains a reservoir of excess ethereal energy and ectoplasm that she can draw upon to improve her *phantom weapon.* This pool has a number of ectoplasmic points equal to half her spiritualist level + her Wisdom modifier. The pool refreshes once per day when the spiritualist refreshes her daily spells." ability2="The phantom blade can spend a number of ectoplasmic points to enhance or modify her *phantom weapon.* As a @HLfree_action, she can spend 1 point to manifest or harbor her weapon. The spiritualist can also spend 2 ectoplasmic points as a @HLfree_action to allow attacks made with her *phantom weapon* to resolve against touch AC for 1 round." swift="She can spend 2 ectoplasmic points to grant the weapon one of the following weapon special abilities for 1 minute: ‹magic-enh/corrosive›, ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/merciful›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, or ‹magic-enh/throwing›." imp11="She can spend 3 ectoplasmic points to grant the weapon one of the following weapon special abilities for 1 minute: ‹magic-enh/corrosive burst›, ‹magic-enh/disruption›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/holy›, ‹magic-enh/icy burst›, ‹magic-enh/shocking burst›, or ‹magic-enh/unholy›." imp15="She can spend 5 ectoplasmic points to grant the weapon one of the following weapon special abilities for 1 minute: ‹magic-enh/brilliant energy›, ‹magic-enh/dancing›, or ‹magic-enh/speed›." special="A phantom blade can spend an appropriate number of points to grant the weapon multiple abilities as one action. A *phantom weapon* cannot have a modified bonus higher than +10."}
::ab[Reshape (Ex)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="A phantom blade can change the weapon type of her *phantom weapon* when she refreshes her daily spells."}
::ab[Phantom Tether (Su)]{l=9 icon=magic swift="A phantom blade can spend 1 ectoplasmic point to instantly recall her *phantom weapon* to her hand or consciousness from as far as 1 mile away."}

::ab[Quick Reshape (Ex)]{l=13 icon=magic ability="A phantom blade can spend 1 ectoplasmic point to reshape her *phantom weapon* as a @HLfull-round_action, 2 ectoplasmic points to reshape her weapon as a @HLstandard_action, 3 ectoplasmic points to reshape her weapon as a @HLmove_action, and 4 ectoplasmic points to reshape her weapon as a @HLswift_action."}

::ab[Etheric Strikes (Sp)]{l=17 icon=lower swift="A phantom blade can spend 1 ectoplasmic point to allow her *phantom weapon* to ignore a number of points of damage reduction equal to twice her Wisdom modifier or twice the weapon's Charisma modifier, whichever is higher."}

::ab[Soul Eater (Su)]{l=19 icon=boost-magic-aid flavor="The phantom blade and her weapon can feast on the energy of departing souls." ability="Each time she kills a living or incorporeal creature with her *phantom weapon,* she can pick one of the following effects: she gains a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls for 1 round, she recovers one 1st- or 2nd-level spell slot, or she gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to the *phantom weapon's* ego (these temporary hit points last until lost or 1 minute, whichever is shorter)." special="The creature killed must have a number of Hit Dice equal to or greater than half the phantom blade's spiritualist level for this to occur."}

plague_eater.name: Plague Eater
description: ## Plague Eater
‹SOURCE Heroes of Golarion/12›

Iobaria has been ravaged by plague for centuries, and many of its dead do not rest. These ghosts often flock around specialized spiritualists called plague eaters. Such a spiritualist bears the weight of many souls within her consciousness, spirits that remember the devastation of disease and seek to protect their host from infections, cleanse others of plague, and use disease itself as a weapon.

::ab[Plague Ward]{icon=power-down-def ability="A plague eater does not bond with a single spirit, but rather a group of spirits gathered around her psychic presence. The spiritualist can meditate on an emotional focus during her 8-hour rest and bond with a spirit from her community with a matching focus. A spirit associated with the chosen emotion links with the spiritualist and acts as her phantom until the plague eater changes her emotional focus with another 8-hour rest. The changing roster of spirits dilutes the bond between the plague eater and her community, and the manifesting phantom has a level 1 lower than the spiritualist's level (minimum level 1)." passive="While a phantom is confined in a plague eater's consciousness (but not while it's fully manifested or banished to the Ethereal Plane), it grants a +4 bonus on saving throws against disease." order=ap special="In addition, the phantom grants the plague eater the ‹feat/Skill Focus› feat in one of the skills determined by the phantom's emotional focus. This skill is chosen by the plague eater the first time she confines the phantom tied to that particular emotional focus. Once this choice is made for a given emotional focus, it cannot be changed." imp12="The saving throw bonus becomes +8." alter="Phantom~shared consciousness"}

::ab[Spiritual Inoculation (Su)]{icon=def l4="Whenever a plague eater is within reach of her ectoplasmic manifested phantom, she gains a +4 circumstance bonus on saving throws against disease." alter="Spiritual interference"}

::ab[Remove Sickness (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic-aid ability="A plague eater's understanding of disease grows to the point where she can cast ‹spell/remove sickness› at will as a spell-like ability with a caster level equal to her spiritualist level." replace="Detect undead"}

::ab[Disfiguring Touch (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="A plague eater can inflict upon others the superficial disfigurement associated with plague by casting ‹spell/disfiguring touch› as a spell-like ability once per day." useInc=spiritualist~4~7 useF="7~1~4" replace="Calm spirit"}

::ab[Remove Disease (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic-aid ability="A plague eater gains the ability to purge disease from herself and others. She gains the ability to cast ‹spell/remove disease› as a spell-like ability three times per day." replace="See invisibility"}

::ab[Fused Well-Being (Su)]{l=10 icon=boost-def passive="A plague eater always gains the benefits of *bonded senses* when her phantom is manifested. She also gains the skill ranks and bonus to resist disease granted by the spirit's emotional focus, even when her phantom is manifested." replace="Fused consciousness"}

::ab[Greater Spiritual Inoculation (Su)]{l=12 icon=def passive="The plague eater becomes immune to disease, including supernatural and magical diseases. In addition, whenever a manifested phantom is in ectoplasmic form, each of the plague eater's allies within the phantom's reach gains a +4 circumstance bonus on saving throws against disease." alter="Greater spiritual interference"}

::ab[Withdraw Affliction (Sp)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="A plague eater can reach into a body, remove an affliction directly from the flesh, and use the diseased matter as a weapon. She can cast ‹spell/withdraw affliction› once per day as a spell-like ability with a caster level equal to her spiritualist level." replace="Call spirit"}

priest_of_the_fallen.name: Priest of the Fallen
description: ## Priest of the Fallen
‹SOURCE Heroes of Golarion/17›

Countless hero-gods have risen and fallen through Iblydos's long history, leaving behind mighty spirits. Those attuned to these fallen hero-gods can become conduits for their restless souls and agents acting out their divine will. The result is a priest of the fallen, a spiritualist who channels her land's legends and provides a vessel for mighty heroes to perform heroic acts once more.

::ab[Mythmaker (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A priest of the fallen can channel many different hero-god phantoms, though only one at a time. These spirits will not suffer being confined in a mortal's consciousness and must be manifested in ectoplasmic or incorporeal form or they return to the Ethereal Plane. They each have an emotional focus depending on their personality and retain some of their hero-god powers, determined by their mythic archetype." usage="A priest of the fallen can channel a hero-god phantom with a ritual that takes 1 minute to perform. A priest of the fallen can channel only one hero-god phantom per 24 hours, but once channeled, a hero-god phantom remains until a new one is channeled." order=au}

::ab[Archmage]{next icon=magic ability="The phantom gains ‹spell/magic missile› and ‹spell/sleep› as spell-like abilities. It can use each spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to its Hit Dice, which it uses in place of a caster level." imp12="It can also cast ‹spell/fireball› and ‹spell/hold person› each once per day."}

::ab[Champion]{next icon=power ability="The phantom gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (slam) as a bonus feat." imp12="It gains ‹feat/Greater Weapon Focus› (slam) and ‹feat/Weapon Specialization› (slam) as bonus feats."}

::ab[Guardian]{next icon=def passive="The phantom gains a +2 natural armor bonus when it manifests in its ectoplasmic form, and a +2 deflection bonus to AC when it manifests in its incorporeal form." imp12="The phantom increases its maximum hit points by twice its number of Hit Dice."}

::ab[Hierophant]{next icon=magic ability="The phantom gains ‹spell/bane› and ‹spell/bless› as spell-like abilities. It can use each spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to its Hit Dice, which it uses in place of a caster level." imp12="It can also cast ‹spell/prayer› and ‹spell/searing light› each once per day."}

::ab[Marshal]{next icon=def-boost standard="The phantom can fill its allies with its triumphant spirit, granting them a +1 morale bonus on saving throws against charm and fear effects and a +1 competence bonus on attack and weapon damage rolls." useMod=Charisma2 useUnit=round useNC imp12="If the phantom is hit by an attack of opportunity, it can use an @HLimmediate_action to allow an ally to make an attack of opportunity against the creature that attacked the phantom if it's within the ally's melee reach."}

::ab[Trickster]{next icon=power-boost ability="The phantom gains the ‹ability/sneak attack› rogue class feature as a rogue of a level equal to the phantom's Hit Dice." useMod=Dex useM imp12="Whenever the phantom is in darkness or ‹rule/dim light›, it can move at full speed without taking a penalty on Stealth checks." alter=Phantom replace="Shared consciousness"}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{l=3 icon=power-aura head flavor="A priest of the fallen can channel her phantom's divine energy." ability="This functions as the cleric's ‹ability/channel energy› class feature, except she heals or deals damage using her spiritualist level - 2 as her cleric level. (1d6 at 3rd level, 2d6 at 5th level, and so on.)" special="The priest of the fallen decides to channel positive or negative energy based on her alignment. A neutral priest of the fallen can channel either positive or negative energy, but once this choice is made, it cannot be changed." useMod=Charisma useM replace="Bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{next icon=power-aura order=usc flavor="Text from the cleric class ability." provokes=No standard="You must present your holy symbol to channel energy. This causes a ‹misc/burst› that affects all creatures of one type (either undead or living) in a 30-foot radius centered on you. This deals or heals (see below) 1d6 damage. You can choose whether or not to include yourself in this effect. Creatures that take damage from channeled energy receive a Will save to halve the damage, with a DC equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› your cleric level + your Charisma modifier." choice="A good cleric (or one who worships a good deity) channels @HLpositive_energy and can choose to deal damage to undead creatures or to heal living creatures. An evil cleric (or one who worships an evil deity) channels @HLnegative_energy and can choose to deal damage to living creatures or to heal undead creatures. A neutral cleric who worships a neutral deity (or one who is not devoted to a particular deity) must choose whether she channels positive or negative energy. Once this choice is made, it cannot be reversed." special="Creatures healed by channeled energy cannot exceed their maximum hit point total - all excess healing is lost." useMod=Cha3 incrementPlain="The damage~1~2" incrementEnd="d6."}

::ab[Phantom Call (Su)]{l=6 icon=power standard="Once per day, a priest of the fallen can summon a fully manifested hero-god phantom from the Ethereal Plane. If she already has a hero-god phantom manifested, that phantom vanishes back to the Ethereal Plane." replace="Phantom recall"}

::ab[Fused Consciousness (Su)]{l=10 icon=info info="The *fused consciousness* ability functions as usual, except the phantom retreats to the Ethereal Plane rather than the priest's consciousness." alter="Fused consciousness"}

::ab[Masterful Faith (Su)]{l=17 icon=magic-learn ability="A priest of the fallen chooses any two ‹ability/cleric »domains› and receives their full benefits, treating her spiritualist level as her cleric level. She adds the domain spells to her spells known." replace="Dual bond"}

::ab[True Legend (Su)]{l=20 icon=power flavor="A priest of the fallen becomes strong enough to confine a phantom in her consciousness." standard="She confines a phantom in her consciousness. On the start of her following turn, she gains access to all standard spiritualist abilities involving a confined phantom." useMod=Wisdom4 useUnit=round alter="Empowered consciousness"}

quintessentialist.name: Quintessentialist
description: ## Quintessentialist
‹SOURCE Distant Realms/53›

When the pressure to grow wars with a personal need for simplicity, a mortal soul can split between extremes, creating two half-beings that rely on one another to survive. The quintessentialist learns to project her best self - her *exemplar* - as an independent being, but in doing so leaves only the weakest and basest aspects behind in her body.

::ab[Unfocused Spellcasting]{icon=down ability="A quintessentialist's sheared mind has difficulty focusing on even the simplest spells. All of her spells have a minimum casting time of 1 round, even if casting a spell would normally take less time, such as a standard action."}

::ab[Exemplar]{icon=power-down order=na2 info="Instead of bonding to the phantom of a dead soul, a quintessentialist forges her best qualities into a spiritual aspect called an *exemplar,* which resides in her subconscious mind at all times rather than on the Ethereal Plane. An *exemplar* uses the quintessentialist's ability scores when fully manifested rather than those of a standard phantom, but otherwise advances as a phantom." ability="The *exemplar* can cast any of the spiritualist's spells while fully manifested, sharing the same spells known and spell slots, and does not suffer from the quintessentialist's *unfocused spellcasting* ability." ability2="A quintessentialist can fully manifest her *exemplar* as a @HLfull-round_action rather than a 1-minute ritual, and she can dismiss it back into her mind as a @HLstandard_action. When fully manifested, a quintessentialist can transfer any equipment she is currently wearing or carrying to her *exemplar,* allowing it to manifest with weapons, armor, and other equipment, but doing so removes these possessions from the quintessentialist. She can likewise grant her *exemplar* any feats she knows, losing access to those feats herself while the *exemplar* remains fully manifested." special="Projecting an *exemplar* is draining. A quintessentialist cannot cast any spells herself and she takes a -2 penalty to all ability scores while her *exemplar* is fully manifested. Every round the *exemplar* remains fully manifested, the quintessentialist takes 1d6 points of damage; this damage cannot be reduced or prevented in any way." alter=Phantom}

scourge.name: Scourge
description: ## Scourge
‹SOURCE Haunted Heroes Handbook/22›

Scourges are students of pain and have a rare connection to tormented and wracked spirits. Most are worshipers of ‹faith/Zon-Kuthon› who are more attracted to his mastery of pain than his lordship over darkness. As a result, scourges are found not only in Nidal, but also in Cheliax, Geb, and Irrisen. Faithful of the Midnight Lord often claim the spirits bound to scourges are tortured souls who begged to be given some boon by their deity, and that having their torment extend into death is a reward, rather than a punishment. Scourges seek to share their phantoms' miseries with all around them, using the spirit's pain as a weapon. A scourge's phantom is a broken and wretched creature, and the torments it suffered in life are reflected in its ghostly or ectoplasmic appearance as wounds, scars, grotesque malformations, and tattered garments.

::ab[Spell Scourge (Su)]{l=4 icon=lower-boost order=ap ability="When a scourge's phantom damages a creature, it causes severe pain, requiring that creature to succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check (DC = 20 + spell level) to use spells, spell-like abilities, and other abilities that require concentration for 1 round." passive="The phantom gains a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls when making attacks of opportunity. Any creature threatened by the scourge's phantom takes a -2 penalty on concentration checks." replace="Spiritual interference"}

::ab[Endure Torment (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def passive="A scourge's phantom gains immunity to pain effects and gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against effects that could cause it to become ‹misc/staggered› or ‹misc/stunned›." replace=Devotion}

::ab[Inflict Pain (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic useInc=spiritualist~4~7 ability="A scourge can use ‹spell/inflict pain› as a spell-like ability. If her phantom is fully manifested, it can also use this ability, though doing so takes the phantom a @HLfull-round_action, and it still counts against the scourge's daily uses of this ability." replace="Calm spirit"}

::ab[Ectoplasmic Swarm (Su)]{l=12 icon=power standard="A scourge with a phantom manifested in ectoplasmic form can command it to break apart in a gruesome display of gore and agony, transforming it into a swarm of Diminutive ectoplasmic organs and viscera. The phantom gains the ‹subtype/swarm› subtype, dealing its unmodified slam damage die as damage for its swarm attack. Its distraction DC is equal to 10 + 1/2 the phantom's Hit Dice + its Constitution modifier." special="In this form, the phantom is too diffuse to use inflict pain or any of its abilities from emotional focus (even passive abilities)." replace="Greater spiritual interference"}

seeker_of_enlightenment.name: Seeker of Enlightenment
description: ## Seeker of Enlightenment
‹SOURCE Disciple's Doctrine/22›

Sometimes a spiritualist is so haunted by a past life's failure to reach enlightenment that the past life is reborn not as a new creature but as a phantom in a spiritualist's consciousness. The spiritualist and phantom work in tandem to reach enlightenment and atone for the past life's failures.

::ab[Echoes of Expertise (Ex)]{icon=boost-def order=fp passive="A seeker of enlightenment gains a bonus equal to her spiritualist level on checks attempted with the two skills determined by her phantom's emotional focus. When the phantom is confined to the seeker of enlightenment's consciousness, it grants a +2 bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects." imp12="The bonus to saving throws increases to +4." free="Once per day, the seeker of enlightenment can attempt a check with any Knowledge skill, including one in which she is not trained, and add her spiritualist level as a bonus on the check." replace="Shared consciousness"}

::ab[Words of the Past (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A seeker of enlightenment's connection with her past lives becomes so strong that she can cast ‹spell/comprehend languages› at will as a spell-like ability, with a caster level equal to her spiritualist level." replace="Detect undead"}

::ab[Karmic Insight (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic flavor="A seeker of enlightenment gains the ability to understand which actions will push her toward ultimate understanding." ability="She can cast ‹spell/augury› as a spell-like ability." useInc=spiritualist~4~7 useF="7~1~4" replace="Calm spirit"}

::ab[Pinpoint Influence (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic flavor="A seeker of enlightenment gains the ability to track objects that are important to her goal of enlightenment." ability="She can cast ‹spell/locate object› as a spell-like ability once per day, with a duration of 10 minutes." replace="See invisibility"}

::ab[Echoes of Mastery (Ex)]{l=10 icon=def-boost ability="A seeker of enlightenment gains the bonus on saving throws from *echoes of expertise* even when her phantom is manifested. Additionally, once per day, she can take 20 on any Knowledge check, including one in which she is not trained." replace="Fused consciousness"}

::ab[Knowledge of the Ancestors (Sp)]{l=16 icon=magic flavor="A seeker of enlightenment learns how to tap into the knowledge of her past lives." ability="She can cast ‹spell/legend lore› as a spell-like ability once per day." replace="Call spirit"}

shadow_caller.name: Shadow Caller
description: ## Shadow Caller
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/50›
