While most spiritualists form a bond with the remnants of a creature's soul, some explore ways to bind ephemeral shadows to their own souls in exchange for power.
::prereq{other="Creatures that don't normally cast shadows (such as vampires) cannot be shadow callers"}

::ab[Shadowcaster]{icon=learn-boost order=np info="A shadow caller adds ‹spell/darkness› and ‹spell/deeper darkness› to their class spell list as 2nd- and 3rd-level spells, respectively." passive="When her shade (see below) inhabits her shadow, the shadow caller's effective caster level is one higher when she casts all spells with the shadow or darkness descriptor." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Shade]{icon=power ability="A shadow caller gains a special kind of phantom known as a shade. A shade functions as a phantom, except as noted below. A shade is from the Shadow Plane instead of the Ethereal Plane. A shade is not confined to the shadow caller's consciousness, but instead inhabits her shadow." alter=Phantom}

::ab[Emotionless (Su)]{head flavor="A shadow caller's shade lacks an emotional focus; it is cold and devoid of all emotions." ability="The shade gains a number of ranks in Intimidate and Stealth equal to its Hit Dice. The shadow caller gains ‹feat/Skill Focus› in each of these skills while the shade inhabits her shadow." info="The shade has ‹misc/good« Reflex and Will» saves›, gains ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes› as a bonus feat, and gains the following abilities." order=an icon=boost-info}

::ab[Weakening Touch (Su)]{next icon=lower-touch order=as ability="A shade's slam attack causes its victim to take a -2 penalty to Strength for 1 round." standard="A shade in incorporeal form can deliver the penalty as a touch attack." special="This penalty does not stack with itself. This is a negative energy effect."}

::ab[Umbral Shroud (Su)]{next icon=aura-boost l7="The the shadow caller's shade can emit a 10-foot aura of swirling shadows that grants all allies 20% ‹rule/concealment› against creatures without darkvision or the ‹umr/see in darkness› ability, and a +4 bonus on Stealth checks."}

::ab[Shadow Caster (Sp)]{next icon=magic l12="Her shade gains the ability to cast either ‹spell/shadow conjuration› or ‹spell/shadow evocation› once per day. It uses its Hit Dice as its caster level, and the DC for these spells equals 10 + 1/2 the shade's Hit Dice + its Charisma modifier. Instead of the normal spell level and percentage of reality for the two spells, the maximum spell level of the duplicated spell is 4th level, and the effects are 40% real." imp18="The maximum spell level improves to 6th level and effects are 60% real."}

::ab[Midnight Shroud (Su)]{next l17="Her shade's *umbral shroud* also lowers the ‹rule/light level› in its range to darkness, regardless of the prevailing conditions; this counts as a 7th-level *darkness* spell for the purpose of adjudicating light and darkness effects." icon=boost}

::ab[Inhabit Shadow (Su)]{icon=info-boost order=np info="A shade resides within a shadow caller's own shadow. When the shade is summoned, the shadow caller does not cast a shadow under any circumstances." passive="While the shade inhabits its master's shadow, the shadow caller gains darkvision 60 feet. If the shadow caller already has darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet. Additionally, the shadow caller gains a +4 circumstance bonus on Stealth checks against creatures without darkvision or the *see in darkness* ability." imp12="This bonus increases to +8." replace="Shared consciousness"}

::ab[Shadow Bound (Su)]{icon=down-info ability="A shadow caller's shade remains stable while adjacent to the shadow caller, or while up to 5 feet away from the shadow caller for every 5 levels the shadow caller possesses. If the shade moves beyond this distance, it is automatically dismissed back to the Shadow Plane (preventing the shade from being summoned back for 24 hours) unless the shadow caller concentrates as per the *etheric tether* ability. However, if both the shadow caller and shade are in areas of total darkness, the shade can move any distance from the shadow caller." special="If the shade is beyond its maximum distance and enters an area of dim or brighter illumination, it immediately returns to the Shadow Plane and cannot be summoned for 24 hours." replace="Etheric tether"}

::ab[Shadow Jump (Su)]{head l=6 icon=magic ability="When a shadow caller's shade inhabits her shadow, she can travel between shadows as per the ‹pclass/shadowdancer«'s› shadow jump ability. Each day, she can travel a total of 40 feet per spiritualist level in this way and, as with a shadowdancer, the distance is rounded to the nearest 10-foot increment." replace="Phantom recall"}

::ab[Shadow Jump (Su)]{next flavor="Relevant text from the shadowdancer class ability." icon=magic order=au ability="A shadowdancer gains the ability to travel between shadows as if by means of a ‹spell/dimension door› spell. The limitation is that the magical transport must begin and end in an area with at least some dim light." usage="A shadowdancer can jump up to a total of 40 feet each day in this way. This amount can be split among many jumps, but each one, no matter how small, counts as a 10-foot increment."}
::ab[See in Darkness (Su)]{l=9 icon=power standard="A shadow caller can gain the ‹umr/see in darkness› universal monster ability." useL=spiritualist useNC replace="See invisibility"}

::ab[Umbral Aura (Su, Sp)]{l=10 icon=def-power order=pa passive="A shadow caller is wreathed in an aura of darkness while her shade inhabits her shadow, gaining 50% ‹rule/concealment› against creatures without darkvision or the *see in darkness* ability. Additionally, the shadow caller's square and all adjacent squares are treated as areas of dim illumination while she benefits from this ability; this second effect counts as a 4th-level *darkness* spell for the purpose of adjudicating light and darkness effects." ability="While her shade inhabits her shadow, the shadow caller can *hide in plain sight* (the ability to use ‹skill/Stealth› to hide, even while being observed), even if an observing creature has darkvision or *see in darkness.*" replace="Fused consciousness"}

::ab[Living Darkness (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-touch-aid order=ps flavor="A shadow caller becomes a creature of absolute darkness." passive="When using her incorporeal bonded manifestation, she is immune to cold and mind-affecting effects." standard="She can use an incorporeal touch attack to deal 20d6 points of negative energy damage. When the shadow caller damages a creature with this ability, she gains 5d6 ‹rule/temporary hit points› (Fortitude half; DC = 20 + her Wisdom modifier), so long as she doesn't already have any temporary hit points gained via this ability). Temporary hit points gained this way disappear after 1 hour." replace="Empowered consciousness"}

soul_warden.name: Soul Warden
description: ## Soul Warden
‹SOURCE Planar Adventures/20›

Normally, the bond a spiritualist shares with his phantom persists for the duration of the spiritualist's life, but in certain rare instances, a spiritualist can guide the phantom hiding within her on to the afterlife, choosing to release the phantom and abandoning many of the classic powers her class normally grants her. When this unusual circumstance happens, the spiritualist retains all the psychic potential from her time serving as a conduit and a vessel, even though she is no longer accompanied by her phantom. While some who would have otherwise become adventuring spiritualists take this as an opportunity to instead choose a normal life, others are galvanized to become wardens of the soulstream, serving ‹faith/Pharasma› and working with ‹family/psychopomp«s› to protect vulnerable souls from those who seek to capture, corrupt, or devour them. At the GM's discretion, a spiritualist might help her phantom move on during play, and if the attempt is successful, she can apply this archetype retroactively, making the appropriate modifications to her class choices as if she had been a soul warden from first level.

::ab[Nosoi Scribe]{l=1 head icon=power order=an ability="A soul warden gains a fledgling nosoi ‹ability/familiar›. Treat this as a ‹monster/raven« familiar› until the soul warden reaches 4th level, at which point it is treated as a ‹monster/nosoi› (as though the soul warden had taken the ‹feat/Improved Familiar› feat)." info="The familiar uses the soul warden's spiritualist level as its Hit Dice for the purpose of determining the effects and duration of its *haunting melody* ability and all other effects related to its HD." imp10="The nosoi scribe gains the ability to use its *touch the spirit world* ability three times per day, instead of once per day." imp12="It also gains the ‹domain/Repose› domain's *ward against death* ability." replace="Phantom~etheric tether~shared consciousness~spiritual interference~fused consciousness~greater spiritual interference"}

::ab[Ward Against Death (Su)]{next flavor="Text from the Respose domain ability." icon=protect standard="You can emit a 30-foot aura that wards against death. Living creatures in this area are immune to all death effects, energy drain, and effects that cause ‹rule/negative levels›. This ward does not remove negative levels that a creature has already gained, but the negative levels have no effect while the creature is inside the warded area." useL=cleric useNC}

::ab[Psychopomp's Bond (Su)]{flavor="A soul warden's bond with her nosoi scribe is stronger than usual for a master and familiar." icon=power l2="She can use *bonded senses* with her nosoi scribe." l6="She can use *phantom recall* to recall her nosoi scribe to her side." alter="Bonded senses~phantom recall"}

::ab[Warding Vessel (Su)]{l=3 icon=protect ability="A soul warden can allow a spirit, soul, or incorporeal creature to hide inside her. While within the soul warden, the warded creature can communicate with the soul warden, and it can be targeted only by effects that could target a creature possessing another creature's body. The warded creature has no other influence over the soul warden or her body." special="If the soul warden is reduced below 0 hit points, the warded creature is expelled." replace="Bonded manifestation"}

::ab[Phantom Elegy (Su)]{icon=aura l8="A soul warden's nosoi scribe learns to sing elegies that build on the soul warden's conduit to her former phantom. This allows it to create an effect identical to the 7th-level *aura* ability of an ‹emotionalfocus/anger›, ‹emotionalfocus/dedication›, ‹emotionalfocus/despair›, ‹emotionalfocus/fear›, ‹emotionalfocus/hatred›, ‹emotionalfocus/jealousy›, or ‹emotionalfocus/zeal› phantom, except that the *phantom elegy* is also a sonic effect and continues only as long as the nosoi scribe sings. Each round of *phantom elegy* uses 1 round of the nosoi scribe's *haunting melody.*" l17="The nosoi scribe can blend any two of the phantom aura abilities into a single *phantom elegy,* although it uses 3 rounds of its *haunting melody* for each round it does so." replace="Bonded manifestation increases~dual bond"}

::ab[Algea Call (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic standard="Once per hour, a soul warden's nosoi scribe can summon countless other nosoi, which flock together to form an ‹monster/algea›. This transformation lasts for 1 minute, after which the nosoi scribe returns unharmed, unless the algea is brought to 0 hit points or killed, in which case the nosoi scribe returns unconscious at 0 hit points. This is a summoning effect." replace="Empowered consciousness"}

totem_spiritualist.name: Totem Spiritualist
description: ## Totem Spiritualist
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/6›

The god callers of Sarkoris had numerous traditions mixing naturalism with the arcane and occult arts. One of the few traditions to survive to the present day is that of the totem spiritualists. These specialized spiritualists call upon phantoms that are manifestations of animals and personifications of nature.

:::ab{title="Phantom Animal" icon=power action=Ability imp7="The totem spiritualist can either *gain an additional phantom animal* or *empower a phantom animal,* as described in the options below." imp12="She can *gain* or *empower* again." imp17="She can *gain* or *empower* again." head replace=Phantom special="A totem spiritualist can release a phantom animal from her service much like a ‹class/druid› releases an animal companion. If a totem spiritualist releases a phantom animal from service, she can gain a new one by performing a ritual requiring 24 uninterrupted hours of meditation in an environment where the animal that the phantom resembles typically lives.~~~A totem spiritualist treats any druid levels she has as effective spiritualist levels to determine the abilities of her phantom animals, but does not treat her spiritualist levels as effective druid levels to determine the abilities of animal companions."}

A totem spiritualist calls upon spirits of nature known as phantom animals to assist her in her journeys. A phantom animal functions like an ‹sidekick/animal companion› except as noted in the descriptions below.

A totem spiritualist begins play with a phantom animal. The phantom animal uses the statistics of an animal companion and follows the standard animal companion progression in regard to Hit Dice, base attack bonus, saving throws, skills, feats, natural armor bonus, Strength and Dexterity bonuses, and ‹ability/bonus tricks›, but not its special abilities. A phantom animal instead gains the special abilities of ‹sidekick/a standard »phantom›. A phantom animal is capable of becoming harbored in the spiritualist's consciousness or becoming fully or partially manifested. A phantom animal is otherwise considered a phantom for the purposes of all feats and abilities.

::ab[Gain an Additional Phantom Animal]{icon=power sub ability="This additional phantom animal uses the appropriate statistics for a phantom animal of its level." special="A totem spiritualist can have only one active phantom animal at a time. Dismissing an active phantom animal to call another requires a ritual that takes 1 minute to perform."}

::ab[Empower a Phantom Animal]{icon=boost sub ability="The first time the phantom animal is empowered, it gains two bonus tricks. The second time it is empowered, the phantom animal gains the hunter's ‹ability/animal focus› class ability, treating the totem spiritualist's spiritualist level as her effective ‹class/hunter› level. The third time it is empowered, the damage dice of the phantom animal's natural attacks increase ‹misc/by one step›."}

::ab[Shared Instincts (Su)]{icon=boost passive="A totem spiritualist doesn't gain Skill Focus feats while a phantom animal is confined in her consciousness. She instead gains a number of bonus ranks in Knowledge (nature) equal to the phantom animal's Hit Dice." alter="Shared consciousness"}

::ab[Animal Senses (Su)]{l=9 icon=power flavor="A totem spiritualist shares her phantom animal's senses." ability="If her phantom animal has any of the following abilities, she gains them whenever the phantom animal is harbored in her consciousness: ‹umr/blindsense›, darkvision, low-light vision, and ‹umr/scent›." replace="See invisibility"}

::ab[Twin Phantoms (Su)]{l=17 icon=boost flavor="The totem spiritualist can call upon two of her phantom animals at once." standard="She can manifest a second phantom animal. The phantom animal remains for a number of rounds per day equal to her spiritualist level and can be dismissed as a @HLfree_action. This duration does not need to be consecutive." replace="Dual bond"}

usher_of_lost_souls.name: Usher of Lost Souls
description: ## Usher of Lost Souls
‹SOURCE Psychic Anthology/7›

Ushers of lost souls are spiritualists who focus on bringing the souls of the dead to their final judgments at the end of the River of Souls. To this end, they specialize in destroying undead and in laying haunts to rest. Skeptics who point out the apparent contradiction that ushers of lost souls use phantoms to aid them are quickly told that their ectoplasmic allies hail from the Ethereal Plane and are not actually undead. Ushers of lost souls are common among the clergy of Pharasma and are seen throughout the Inner Sea region.

::ab[Etheric Channel (Su)]{head flavor="An usher of lost souls can focus ethereal energies to channel waves of disruption." icon=aura ability="The spiritualist can ‹ability/channel positive energy›. She channels energy like a cleric of her spiritualist level, except the channeled energy can be used only to harm undead or haunts. This otherwise functions as *channel energy* for the purposes of feats and abilities. The spiritualist does not require a holy symbol to use this ability." useMod=Charisma3 useM}

::ab[Channel Energy (Su)]{next flavor="Relevant text from the cleric class ability." icon=aura provokes=No standard="You cause a ‹misc/burst› that affects all creatures of one type (either undead or living) in a 30-foot radius centered on you. This deals 1d6 damage. Creatures that take damage from channeled energy receive a Will save to halve the damage, with a DC equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› your cleric level + your Charisma modifier." incrementPlain="The damage~1~2" incrementEnd="d6."}

::ab[Spirit Senses (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="An usher of lost souls gains a bonus equal to half her spiritualist level on Perception checks to detect haunts and incorporeal creatures and on Sense Motive checks to determine whether a creature is possessed, under the effects of an enchantment or curse, or otherwise magically controlled." replace="Bonded senses"}

::ab[Disrupting Phantom (Ex)]{icon=melee l4="An usher of lost souls's phantom gains the ability to directly harm haunts. The phantom can make an attack while within a haunt's area against an AC equal to 10 + the haunt's caster level. On a hit, the phantom deals its slam damage to the haunt; the haunt is immune to critical hits from the phantom." l12="Her phantom can deal critical hits to a haunt, and the critical threat range for its slam attacks against haunts increases to 19-20." replace="Spiritual interference~greater spiritual interference"}

::ab[Locate Haunts (Su)]{l=9 icon=power flavor="An usher of lost souls can locate inactive haunts." standard="She can enhance her vision to detect haunts within 60 feet of her. A haunt's area glows with a bright light for an untriggered haunt or dim light for a haunt that is waiting to reset." useL=spiritualist~10 useUnit=minute useNC=10 replace="See invisibility"}

::ab[Disruptive Influence (Su)]{l=14 icon=magic flavor="The usher of lost souls can put spirits to rest." ability="She can use one of her uses of *calm spirit* to instead cast ‹spell/purge spirit› as a spell-like ability. She can expend two uses of her *etheric channel* to cast either of these spells as a spell-like ability." replace="Spiritual bond"}

ward_spiritualist.name: Ward Spiritualist
description: ## Ward Spiritualist
‹SOURCE Wilderness Origins/18›

While many spiritualists are bound to a departed spirit, a condition more likely to be the result of chance or tragedy than a career choice, there are some individuals who seek out this life. A ward spiritualist ventures into the wilderness and petitions a ‹family/kami› to help her in her travels, either taking an existing ward with her or becoming the kami's new ward.

::ab[Kami Phantom (Su)]{icon=down info="The ward spiritualist's phantom is less powerful than most, but it has an innate connection to its ward. The ward spiritualist's phantom is a kami phantom, functioning as normal for a ‹sidekick/phantom›, except that its base attack bonus is equal to three-quarters its Hit Dice (as a ‹class/rogue›) and its Hit Dice are d8s instead of d10s. Additionally, a kami phantom cannot manifest as incorporeal, only ectoplasmic." alter=Phantom}

:::ab{title="Ward Implement (Su)" action=Ability icon=power alter=Spellcasting replace="Bonded senses~phantom recall~greater spiritual interference" repeatAt="She gains a focus power for her implement school.~2~6~10" special="She must meet the prerequisites of these focus powers, treating her spiritualist level as her occultist level for focus power prerequisites. She also treats her spiritualist level as her occultist level for feat prerequisites."}

The ward spiritualist gains a single implement school of her choice, as an ‹class/occultist›, along with its resonant focus power and base focus power. The ward spiritualist does not gain additional spells from her ward implement, and it always starts each day with mental focus invested equal to half her class level (minimum 1).

When she gains her ward implement, the ward spiritualist must choose either to have a physical implement or to be the implement herself. If she chooses to have a physical implement, her spiritualist spells include it as a focus component, and she can change what item is her implement when she regains her spells at the start of each day. Her implement must be an item that can be held in one hand, and it cannot be a manufactured weapon. If the ward spiritualist chooses to be her own ward implement, her spiritualist spells always have a somatic component in addition to their other components; this component does not become an emotion component.

She can remove the focus or somatic component of her spells, but this requires a ‹rule/concentration› check with a DC equal to 15 + twice the spell level, which is increased for a thought component as normal.

::ab[Merged Manifestation (Su)]{l=3 icon=power head ability="The ward spiritualist learns how to merge her kami phantom with her ward implement. This functions as *bonded manifestation,* except that she does not choose ectoplasmic or incorporeal. Instead, she gains different benefits depending on whether she has a physical implement or is her own implement."}

::ab[Physical Implement]{sub icon=boost ability="If she has a physical implement, when using her *merged manifestation,* her implement functions as a one-handed ‹misc/improvised weapon› of appropriate size, and she does not take the usual penalties associated with wielding an improvised weapon. Additionally, it is considered to have a +1 enhancement bonus for the duration of the *merged manifestation.*" increment="The enchantment bonus~3~5" imp8="It deals damage as a weapon ‹misc/one size category larger›." imp18="It now deals damage as a weapon two size categories larger." imp13="Her implement also gains the benefits of ‹spell/spirit-bound blade› for the duration of the *merged manifestation.*"}

::ab[Is Her Own Implement]{sub icon=melee ability="If she is her own implement, when using her *merged manifestation,* she gains the *unarmed strike* class feature as a 1st level ‹class/unchained monk›." imp8="Her unarmed strike damage is that of a 4th-level unchained monk, and she gains *flurry of blows* as a 1st-level unchained monk." imp13="Her unarmed damage is that of an 8th-level unchained monk, and her unarmed strikes gain the benefits of *spirit-bound blade* for the duration of the *merged manifestation.*" imp18="Her unarmed damage is that of a 12th level unchained monk, and she can use *flurry of blows* as an 11th-level unchained monk."}

:::ab{next title="Unarmed Strike (Ex)" flavor="Text from the unchained monk class ability." icon=melee action=Ability special="Your unarmed strike is treated as both a manufactured weapon and a natural weapon for the purpose of spells and effects that enhance or improve either manufactured weapons or natural weapons. The damage of your strike is determined by your size and level, shown on the chart below."}

You gain ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat. Your attacks can be with fists, elbows, knees, and feet. This means that you can make unarmed strikes with your hands full. There is no such thing as an off-hand attack when you're striking unarmed. You can apply your full Strength bonus on damage rolls for all your unarmed strikes. Your unarmed strikes deal lethal damage, although you can choose to deal nonlethal damage with no penalty on the attack roll. You can make this choice while grappling as well.
| Level | Damage (Small Monk) | Damage (Medium) | Damage (Large) |

::ab[Flurry of Blows (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the unchained monk class ability." icon=melee fullround="You can make a flurry of blows as a full-attack action. When doing this, you can make one additional attack at your highest base attack bonus. This additional attack stacks with the bonus attacks from ‹spell/haste› and other similar effects. When using this ability, you can make these attacks with any combination of your unarmed strikes and weapons that have the monk special weapon quality. You take no penalty for using multiple weapons when making a flurry of blows, but you do not gain any additional attacks beyond what's already granted by the flurry for doing so. (You can still gain additional attacks from a high base attack bonus, from this ability, and from *haste* and similar effects)." imp11="You can now make two additional attacks at your highest base attack bonus during your flurry of blows. This still stacks with *haste* and similar effects."}

zeitgeist_binder.name: Zeitgeist Binder
description: ## Zeitgeist Binder
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/51›

Not all spiritualists deal in the souls of creatures that refuse to pass into death. Zeitgeist binders channel the emotional connections of a group of people into a unique phantom that personifies a community.

::ab[Zeitgeist (Su)]{icon=info info="A zeitgeist binder's phantom is called a zeitgeist. In addition to an emotion, a zeitgeist is tied to one ‹rule/the>aspect of its» settlement<_stat_block›: corruption, crime, economy, law, lore, or society. In order to select an aspect, that aspect of the settlement must have a positive modifier (for instance, a settlement with a negative lore modifier or a modifier of 0 could not manifest a lore zeitgeist). Although a zeitgeist personifies a single chosen settlement, a zeitgeist binder can call it anywhere, but outside the bounds of its settlement, it loses the benefits of its *settlement aspect* and *settlement avatar* abilities." alter=Phantom}

::ab[Settlement Aspect (Sp)]{head icon=magic ability="A zeitgeist binder gains a number of spell-like abilities tied to her zeitgeist's settlement aspect. Other than being based on Wisdom instead of Charisma, these work the same way as the ‹arc-spiritualist/fractured mind«'s› emotional power ability. The *masterwork transformation* spell-like ability does not ignore the material component." replace="Detect undead~calm spirit~see invisibility~call spirit"}
::ab[Corruption]{next icon=magic s5="Conditional favor" s7="False belief" s9="Charm person" s16="Mass suggestion"}
::ab[Crime]{next icon=magic s5="Crime of opportunity" s7="Pilfering hand" s9="Demand offering" s16="Crime wave"}
::ab[Economy]{next icon=magic s5="Crafter's fortune" s7="Masterwork transformation" s9="Beguiling gift" s16="Major creation"}
::ab[Law]{next icon=magic s5="Detect chaos" s7="Arrow of law" s9=Peacebond s16="Mark of justice"}
::ab[Lore]{next icon=magic s5="Object reading" s7=Hypercognition s9="Heightened awareness" s16="Legend lore"}
::ab[Society]{next icon=magic s5="Cultural adaptation" s7=Rumormonger s9="Share language" s16="Coordinated effort"}

::ab[Settlement Avatar (Su)]{l=2 icon=magic-power flavor="The zeitgeist becomes a living avatar of the settlement." standard="The zeitgeist can project its senses out into the settlement, perceiving the general pulse of its chosen aspect throughout the whole settlement as well as anything involving its aspect that is occurring within 50 feet per spiritualist level. The sensing works like ‹spell/enter image›, so the zeitgeist attempts a Perception check at a - 10 penalty to notice any particular activity within range." useL=spiritualist useNC imp10="The zeitgeist can project its senses to feel its settlement aspect at will. Additionally, the zeitgeist binder gains a bonus ‹main/teamwork feat› as long as she is in her zeitgeist's settlement. The zeitgeist binder must meet the teamwork feat's prerequisites, and she grants the feat as a bonus feat to her zeitgeist, even if it doesn't meet the prerequisites." replace="Bonded senses~fused consciousness"}
